title: HfArgumentParser argparse abbreviation conflict
problem_description: When HfArgumentParser (from transformers) is used alongside standard argparse.ArgumentParser, argument prefix abbreviation can cause collisions. For example, --model_name_or_path (from a HF model config dataclass) and --mode (from a custom argparse) both start with --mo. Argleparser's default allow_abbrev=True causes --mo to be ambiguous, crashing the script before any NPU computation begins.
rough_fix_approach: Two complementary fixes: (1) Pass allow_abbrev=False to argparse.ArgumentParser to disable prefix matching. (2) Construct HF dataclass arguments (e.g., MIMOModelArguments) directly in code rather than parsing them through HfArgumentParser, avoiding the dual-parser scenario entirely.

involved_code_files.role: Entry inference script — argument parsing with HfArgumentParser and argparse

reasoning: This pattern affects any HuggingFace-based project that mixes HfArgumentParser (for model/training args) with custom argparse arguments. While not NPU-specific, it blocks migration scripts from starting, making it a hard dependency for successful migration. The fix is mechanical and generalizes across all HF model families. Lower confidence than candidate-001 because the exact parameter names that collide vary by project.
